# CircleCI CLI

Display the status of a CircleCI event and its workflows.

When called without arguments, the project and branch are inferred from
the current git repository's remote and checked-out branch.

Pass an event UUID to look up a specific event.

JSON fields: id, phase, outcome, current_outcome, branch, revision,
             created_at, errors[].type/message,
             workflows[].id/name/phase/outcome/current_outcome/duration/
             jobs[].id/name/phase/outcome/current_outcome/type

For more information about output formatting flags, see `circleci help formatting`.

## Usage

`circleci event get [<event-id>] [flags]`

## Flags

| Flag                  | Description                                                   |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `-b, --branch string` | Branch name (defaults to current branch)                      |
| `--jq string`         | Process values from the response using jq syntax              |
| `--json`              | Output as JSON                                                |
| `--project string`    | Project slug (e.g. gh/org/repo); used for latest-event lookup |

## Inherited Flags

| Flag                  | Description                                                  |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `-c, --config string` | path to config file (default: ~/.config/circleci/config.yml) |
| `--debug`             | enable debug logging                                         |
| `-q, --quiet`         | suppress informational output; data on stdout is unaffected  |

## Examples

- Get the latest event for the current branch: 
  `circleci event get`
- Get an event by UUID: 
  `circleci event get 5034460f-c7c4-4c43-9457-de07e2029e7b`
- Output as JSON for scripting: 
  `circleci event get --json`

## Learn More

- Use `circleci <command> <subcommand> --help` for more information about a command.
- Read the manual at <https://circleci.com/docs/local-cli>
- Support at <https://github.com/CircleCI-Public/circleci-cli/issues>
